top_fines.title: Speeding +20 km/h in community safety zone

faq.question: Must I stop for a school bus in Canada?
faq.question_de: Muss ich in Kanada für Schulbusse anhalten?
faq.answer: When red lights flash and the stop arm is out, traffic in BOTH directions must stop (except on divided highways in some provinces). Fines are heavy—often C$400–C$2,000 plus demerit points.
faq.answer_de: Bei roten Blinklichtern und Stoppschild müssen BEIDE Richtungen anhalten (außer geteilte Straßen in manchen Provinzen). Hohe Bußgelder—oft C$400–C$2.000 plus Punkte.

faq.question: Can I turn right on red in Montreal?
faq.question_de: Darf ich in Montreal bei Rot rechts abbiegen?
faq.answer: No. Right turn on red is prohibited on the entire Island of Montreal unless a specific sign permits it. This differs from most of Canada.
faq.answer_de: Nein. Rechtsabbiegen bei Rot ist auf der ganzen Insel Montreal verboten, außer ausdrücklich erlaubt. Anders als in most of Kanada.

faq.question: Are winter tires mandatory in Canada?
faq.answer: Mandatory in Quebec (Dec 1–Mar 15) and on some BC mountain routes. Strongly recommended elsewhere. All-season tires are not winter tires.
faq.answer_de: Pflicht in Quebec (1. Dez–15. März) und auf manchen BC-Bergstraßen. Sonst empfohlen. Allwetterreifen sind keine Winterreifen.

faq.question: How does Highway 407 ETR work in Ontario?
faq.answer: 407 ETR is fully electronic—no booths. Cameras bill your plate. Rental cars may charge admin fees; register plate or use a transponder.
faq.answer_de: 407 ETR ist rein elektronisch—keine Schranken. Kameras berechnen Kennzeichen. Mietwagen verlangen oft Gebühren.

faq.question: What is the drink-drive limit in Canada?
faq.answer: 0.08% BAC is the criminal limit nationwide. Many provinces impose penalties from 0.05% (warn range/suspension). Zero for novice drivers in most provinces.
faq.answer_de: 0,08 % BAC ist strafrechtliche Grenze bundesweit. Viele Provinzen ahnden ab 0,05 %. Null für Fahranfänger.

faq.question: What is Canada's Move Over law?
faq.answer: Slow down and move over a lane when passing stopped emergency vehicles, tow trucks, or roadside workers with flashing lights. Fines vary by province.
faq.answer_de: Langsamer fahren und Spur wechseln bei Einsatzfahrzeugen, Abschleppern oder Straßenarbeitern mit Blinklicht.

faq.question: Do I need an IDP to drive in Canada?
faq.question_de: Brauche ich einen IDP in Kanada?
faq.answer: Valid licences from many countries are accepted for short visits. IDP recommended if licence is not in English or French. Carry passport.
faq.answer_de: Gültige Führerscheine vieler Länder für Kurzbesuche OK. IDP empfohlen wenn nicht Englisch/Französisch. Pass mitführen.

source_entries.title: Ontario Ministry of Transportation
source_entries.notes: Provincial rules vary; Ontario used as reference.

summary: Driving in Canada is similar to the US, but with metric units (km/h). Winter driving conditions can be severe. Right turn on red allowed unless posted (except Island of Montreal).
common_traps: Right turn on red is prohibited on the entire Island of Montreal.
School bus flashing lights: traffic both ways must stop.
Move Over Law: slow down/move over for emergency vehicles.

speed_limits.notes: Varies by province. Trans-Canada Highway can be 90-110 km/h.

alcohol_limit.notes: 0.05% warns/suspension in many provinces (e.g., BC, Ontario). 0.08% is criminal offense.

rules.priority_rules: Right of way to vehicles already in roundabout.

tolls.notes: Few toll roads (e.g., 407 ETR in Ontario is electronic only).

rules.parking_rules: Winter parking bans common for snow removal.
rules.winter_rules: Winter tires mandatory in Quebec (Dec 1 - Mar 15) and recommended elsewhere.

rental_and_idp_notes: Most licenses accepted for short visits. IDP recommended if not in English/French.

summaries.title: Speeding
summaries.summary: Limits and fines vary by province. Photo radar common in BC, AB, QC.
summaries.summary_de: Limits und Strafen je Provinz. Blitzer in BC, AB, QC üblich.

summaries.title: Distracted driving
summaries.summary: Handheld phone use banned in all provinces.
summaries.summary_de: Handy am Steuer in allen Provinzen verboten.

categories.title: Distracted driving
categories.description: Ontario reference amounts; other provinces differ.

regional_variations.notes: French traffic signs. No right on red on Montreal Island. Winter tires mandatory Dec–Mar.
Lower speed limits in rain. Immediate roadside prohibitions from 0.05% BAC.

highlights.title: 0.05% warn range
highlights.description: BC can suspend at 0.05% without criminal charge.
highlights.description_de: BC kann ab 0,05 % sperren ohne Strafverfahren.
